item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly feel like this would have worked better if the D.B. Cooper reference was implicit rather than explicit, because presenting this as the solution to a major unsolved crime is an anticlimax, to say the least.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image appears to be from here, with no indication it was released under a license compliant with the wiki's (CC BY-SA 3.0). Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved the D. B. Cooper reference, probably cause I got it at "cascade mountains" and "parachute".

All you who associated it with "bag of devouring", not everybody plays D&D.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Possible variant of this SCP that only eats socks and tends to only occupy the inside of laundry machines.
+1

item.title: So this is where the SCP-914 Stuff goes
item.content:encoded:
For those of you who dont know SCP-914 makes a lot of anomalous stuff that is potentially dangerous.
SCP-914

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of an SCP explanation for D.B. Cooper's disappearance, but "he got bit by a mimic and we 100% definitely found him and he's regular dead" is a pretty dissatisfying take on that concept.

item.title: Outdated
item.content:encoded:
The Special Containment procedures seem REALLY intense for utterly no reason. Why an airlock? Why a bio-scan all the time? If 668 can be locked in a box and left alone why can't this? Also, as other commenters have pointed out, this article uses some weird mix and match censoring, and the media's name for D.B. Cooper as opposed to the alias he really used.

-1 until this gets a clean-up
excessive testing leads to excessive tears

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I like it. Yeah, a Bag of Devouring is what I thought of, and I'm pretty sure a lot of PCs use it as a garbage disposal too. That doesn't make it bad imo. This is the rare case in which a classic d&d item is actually suitable as an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would it be OK for me to edit the formatting surrounding the image on this page? It's huge, and looks really blurry and unprofessional.

This skip is one of my old favorites, and I'd rather it were kept in nice shape.

Edit: Actually, the whole thing could probably use a bit of a rework. I might see about doing that after I put up my current project.
Cheers. Not the most ambitious SCP, but it's neat, and you think being crosslinked from 35 would've gotten it more exposure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We know that this bag is connected to a mouth. We know that most mouths are connected to throats. We know (thanks to 835) that there is little or no upper limit to the length a throat can be.
Therefore, I propose the following: One day, all this shit is going to reach its stomach.
And who knows what will happen then?

+1 for the intriguing and mildly worrying anomaly.

item.title: The connection does it for me. . . .
item.content:encoded: The fact that the bag-mouth will one day probably vomit, along with the fact that it is used to dispose of the material produced by SCP-035 and god knows what other SCP by-products, is what really does it for me here. I like the implication that one day this thing will backfire horribly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't it be stated that this object should only be allowed to consume hazardous materials and declassified clerical data? Just in case whatever is on the other side happens to be looking at what goes through?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would the article not refer to him as Dan Cooper? (Dan cooper being his actual alias while D.B. Cooper is a media miscommunication).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still don't understand why this is Euclid and not Safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hasn't aged well. Also, it's too useful to the SCPF, with no visible downside.

Downvote.

item.title: Re: So Scp 19 101 bad Idea
item.content:encoded: I think it's a great idea!

item.title: So Scp 19 101 bad Idea
item.content:encoded: So you would not support using SCP-101 to dispose of SCP-019-2 specimens

item.title: Re: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Fine by me.

item.title: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Requesting consensus from other Mods/Admins to go through and strip out the Imperial measurements, since they're distracting and only rough approximations of the metric anyway. Also, may I replace that "approved by [EXPUNGED]" with O5-blackboxes? It just bugs me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All the measurements really bog this one down

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Me neither. And literally dumping shit into an extra-dimensional mouth seems incredibly bonkers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand why D.B. Cooper needs to be in this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, it may not seem to react, but I doubt whatever's on the end of that mouth is very pleased at being fed sewage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first thing I thought when I read this SCP was, "this sounds a lot like something from D&D…"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Continuing to feed it seems more dangerous than educational to me. Additionally, using it as a garbage disposal that takes Class 3 personnel and a 10 minute airlock cycle just doesn't make much sense. Also, the Bag of Devouring aspect is very D&D.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It has been tested. We know how it works. All the foundation needs to know is known. 914 and 682 are different, because part of the creep is that we dont understand them. This, as I pointed out earlier, is so easy to understand that it is found as a reasonably common cursed item in games.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, the Foundation fucks with SCPs all the time. That's where most of the information in the descriptions comes from: Tossing stuff at SCPs and seeing what happens. The Foundation doesn't just keep things secured, it tries to understand it. Sometimes, if the item is understood well enough, the Foundation even uses it. That's why there are so many researchers.

That said, most SCPs don't need experiment logs. Sometimes they add to the concept, like 914 or 682. Most of the time, you can just put the information in the description and be done with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, test it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am not asking to kill it, just explore their possibilities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When you dissect the frog it dies, Caldwell. Articles like 914 and 682 are special in that dissecting them a bit adds more to them, but even they have limits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus christ, you just don't get it do you? Tests break the whole concept of the Foundation (eg. locking things up and NOT FUCKING WITH THEM). The 682 and 914 logs are bad enough already. We do not need a page like that for every SCP. At this point, any kind of extended testing is discouraged because it's against the site tone, it takes up too much space and drops the creep factor to near-zero levels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -snip-

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: NO! Articles do not need more tests! Caldwell, seriously, stop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Test this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The actual problem with "D&D items" is that they tend to be too useful, and not so creepy. Yeah, it's a bag of devouring, but it's different from that in certain subtle ways, such as mimicry. Instead of a cursed item, it's actually predatory.

However, it is very useful in getting ride of unwanted materials, so it may be something that falls under "your mileage may vary." Which is why the rating module is there. <grin>

I didn't write it, and I did upvote it, myself. Everyone's got their thoughts, and it's good to exchange them, you know?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't think we where supposed to put items for Dungeons and Dragons here. This is the Bag of Devouring.

http://www.d20srd.org/srd/magicItems/cursedItems.htm#bagofDevouring
